developer.comment: Repo is anomalyco/opencode; project originated with the SST team and was rebranded under Anomaly.

first-release.comment: Public terminal release in June 2025 (repo created April 2025); checked 2026-06-05.

status.comment: Actively developed, frequent releases; ~170k GitHub stars as of 2026-06-05.

license.comment: MIT License per GitHub repo metadata.

interface.comment: Primary terminal TUI/CLI; IDE extension runs opencode in the integrated terminal of VS Code and forks; web/share interface for sessions. Docs describe a desktop app but the IDE page clarifies the extension is terminal-based rather than a standalone desktop GUI.

host-ides.comment: Runs in the terminal; VS Code extension also covers Cursor, Windsurf, VSCodium (VS Code forks). Shared sessions viewable in browser. No native JetBrains/Neovim extension.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Fundamentally a CLI tool; also ships an editor extension that launches it in the integrated terminal.

default-model-provider.comment: No hardcoded default; supports 75+ providers via Models.dev. Recommends models from Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, etc.; optional OpenCode Zen gateway is also available.

model-choice.comment: 75+ providers; model selected via --model flag, config, or last-used.

bring-your-own-key.comment: Users add provider credentials via /connect; BYOK is the primary usage model.

local-model-support.comment: Supports local providers such as LM Studio (e.g. lmstudio/... model ids); compatible with local frameworks.

multi-file-editing.comment: Build agent has full edit/file tools to apply coordinated edits across files.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Build agent chains edit/run/bash tools to complete multi-step tasks; subagents can be delegated specific tasks.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Has a bash tool gated by the permission system; commands can also inject shell output.

mcp-support.comment: Documented support for local and remote MCP servers, with OAuth and per-agent enable/disable.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Distinguishes primary agents and subagents; subagents are invoked for specific tasks and can be defined per project/global.

codebase-indexing.comment: No documented semantic/embeddings index; the agent reads files on demand via its file/grep tools rather than building a persistent index.

planning-mode.comment: Built-in Plan agent restricts edits/bash for planning and analysis before switching to Build.

multimodal-input.comment: Supports attaching files including images (@file syntax); image input depends on the selected model. Treated as true given documented file-attachment support, though it is model-dependent.

browser-computer-use.comment: No built-in browser/GUI driving. Browser-style capabilities would require an external MCP server rather than a native feature.

operating-mode.comment: Conversational TUI with per-action permission prompts (supervised); permissions can be set to allow for more autonomous runs. No inline tab completion.

inline-completion.comment: Terminal/TUI agent with no as-you-type tab autocomplete.

background-async-execution.comment: opencode has a client/server architecture and a server mode, but documentation does not clearly establish dispatch-and-walk-away async task execution; left null pending confirmation (checked 2026-06-05).

persistent-memory.comment: Sessions persist and can be resumed; AGENTS.md project/global rules carry context across sessions.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: Intro docs describe undo/redo functionality to revert agent changes within a session.

git-integration.comment: Agent can run git via the bash tool and is git-aware (AGENTS.md committed to git); diffs shown in TUI.

pr-creation.comment: No dedicated PR feature, but can open PRs by running gh/git commands via the bash tool or custom commands.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md (project and global) plus legacy CLAUDE.md; custom instruction files configurable in opencode.json.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Extensible via custom commands, agent skills, custom agents, and MCP servers.

permission-model.comment: Granular per-tool permissions set to ask/allow/deny, with glob patterns; defaults to prompting for approval.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Open-source tool runs entirely on the user's machine with local models or self-managed provider keys; no vendor backend required.

privacy-mode.comment: Runs locally and uses the user's own provider keys (or local models), so the opencode vendor does not collect or train on code; actual training behavior depends on the chosen model provider.

enterprise-controls.comment: No SSO/RBAC/audit-log governance documented for the open-source tool; Zen offers team workspaces (free beta) but not formal enterprise controls.

pricing-model.comment: The tool itself is free and open-source; you pay your model provider (BYOK). Optional OpenCode Zen gateway is pay-as-you-go usage-based with some free models.

free-tier.comment: The agent is free to run; only model usage costs apply, and Zen offers some free models.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Free, open-source tool with no subscription; core coding use is covered at $0 (model API/usage costs are separate).

maturity.comment: Polished, widely adopted (~170k stars), frequent releases; still rapidly evolving so not a full 5.

community-size.comment: ~170k GitHub stars as of 2026-06-05; one of the most popular open-source coding agents.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: MCP servers, agent skills, custom commands and custom agents form a meaningful extensibility ecosystem.

project-memory-files.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md (project + global ~/.config/opencode/AGENTS.md); CLAUDE.md supported as legacy for Claude Code compatibility.

memory-scopes.comment: Global (per-user) and project-root AGENTS.md; project rules apply in the directory and its sub-directories with hierarchical lookup, covering folder scope. No enterprise-policy scope documented.

scoped-rules-files.comment: AGENTS.md is always-on; additional instruction files can be configured with glob patterns (e.g. packages/*/AGENTS.md), giving path-based scoping.

auto-memory.comment: Memory is via manually maintained AGENTS.md; no documented mechanism for the agent to autonomously write cross-session memories.

skill-support.comment: Native Agent Skills (SKILL.md files) loaded on demand via a skill tool; also custom slash commands.

skill-invocation.comment: Skills are agent/model-callable via the skill tool; custom commands are user-callable via /command in the TUI.

always-on-surface.comment: Progressive disclosure: only skill name + description are listed in the skill tool by default; full SKILL.md content is loaded when invoked.

skill-distribution.comment: Skills are markdown files placed in .opencode/skills/<name>/SKILL.md or the global config dir; no official marketplace/registry, so distributed by manual file copy or git.

sandboxing-technology.comment: No kernel sandbox, container, or VM documented; bash/tool actions run with the user's full privileges and safety is enforced purely by the allow/ask/deny permission engine. The external_directory/edit path rules confine writes by glob but provide no OS-level isolation. Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-granularity.comment: Permission keys are per-tool (read, edit, bash, webfetch, websearch, etc.); bash supports per-command and command-prefix glob rules (e.g. "git *": "allow", "rm *": "deny"); edit/external_directory take per-path glob rules; read vs edit are gated separately (read-write distinction). No per-domain network host allowlist is documented. Checked 2026-06-12.

rule-precedence.comment: Rules are evaluated by pattern match with the last matching rule winning; the recommended pattern is a catch-all "*" first and more specific rules after, giving firewall-style user-controlled ordered evaluation. Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-configurability.comment: User authors allow/ask/deny rules (global, per-tool, per-pattern, per-agent) in opencode.json or via OPENCODE_PERMISSION; no code-level permission hook/callback that inspects an action is documented, so it stops at declarative rules. Checked 2026-06-12.

sensitive-file-protection.comment: Out of the box read denies *.env and *.env.* (while allowing *.env.example), so secret files are protected by default without the user authoring a deny rule. Checked 2026-06-12.

persistent-allowlist.comment: The TUI "always" approval whitelists matching patterns only for the rest of the current session and is not persisted across restarts; durable allowlisting requires manually editing opencode.json, which is configuration rather than a remembered approval. Checked 2026-06-12.

unsupervised-mode.comment: Setting "permission": "allow" (globally or via OPENCODE_PERMISSION) runs without any approval prompts, described as the equivalent of Claude Code's --dangerously-skip-permissions for unattended/autonomous use. Checked 2026-06-12.

network-access-control.comment: Outbound access via the webfetch/websearch tools is gated by the same allow/ask/deny permission rules (set per-tool, e.g. "webfetch": "ask"); default is allow and there is no built-in per-domain allowlist, so egress control is user-configurable rather than blocked or host-scoped by default. Checked 2026-06-12.

default-permission-posture.comment: Out of the box opencode allows all operations without approval - bash commands and file edits run automatically (doom_loop and external_directory default to ask, and *.env reads are denied). The only carve-outs keep this short of fully unrestricted, but edits and commands are not prompted by default. Checked 2026-06-12.
